text.title: Agentic Commerce Future Trends Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Agentic Commerce Future Trends application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Score the business on six axes (trust, API/machine-readable, NAP consistency, review authority, structured-data coverage, task-completion content). Missing an axis = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Selective scoring hides the weakest link, which is what AI agents will see.

rule.statement: Each axis carries a typed score (0-5) and a one-line evidence citation referencing a specific URL or doc.
rule.rationale: Untyped scores are folklore; evidence cites make findings defensible.

rule.statement: Recommendations are tagged with a budget bucket (S/M/L) and a quarter. No vague 'we should...'.
rule.rationale: Roadmap without budget tags does not move past the briefing.

rule.statement: NAP + descriptions are sampled across ≥3 platforms (Google, Yelp/G2, niche directory). Drift >5% is flagged P0.
rule.rationale: AI agents reconcile multiple sources; drift creates ambiguity that lowers ranking.

rule.statement: Report carries semver + last_reviewed. Re-run quarterly or on a major business change.
rule.rationale: Agentic commerce evolves fast; old reports mislead investment.

rule.statement: Content library is partitioned by task-completion vs informational; the task-completion slice has its own coverage score.
rule.rationale: AI agents prioritise content that completes a task, not generic guides.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
